items.back: STARS may be used for: identifying aircraft with discrete beacon codes, maintaining target identity, and performing handoffs between controllers. All terminal STARS procedures apply to FUSION target.
STARS does NOT relieve the controller of responsibility for: proper identification, maintenance of identity, handoff of the correct target with alphanumeric data, and separation of aircraft.
Tracking, tagging, handoff, altitude information, coordination, ground speed, and identification.

(a) Inform all positions before terminating/reinstating; transfer/terminate flight data. (b) Inform interfaced facilities of shutdowns. (c) Initiate track/tag on all aircraft; use automated handoff. (d) Assigned altitude must be current; use climb/descent arrows. (e) FUSION mode: display assigned/pilot altitude when level. (f) Auto altitude readout may be used across positions with prearranged coordination.

Use two-letter ICAO or three-letter designators (single letter alpha if system limited). Use inhibit/select per local directives for maximum equipment use. Info displayed per national orders and local directives. During ASR outage with MLAT inop: tower enables ADS-B indicator on TDW.

When CA/MCI alert: evaluate without delay, take appropriate action. If another controller involved: coordinate (not required if immediate action). Suppress: for specific alerts. Inhibit: ONLY for aircraft where approved separation criteria don't apply (practice intercepts, air shows). Acknowledgement = action taken. Cannot suppress/inhibit for another position without coordination.

Inhibit MSAW processing of VFR aircraft and aircraft that cancel IFR flight plans, unless pilot specifically requests otherwise. Low altitude alert may be suppressed; suppress = acknowledgment that action has/will be taken.

Use ONLY when data block overlap in holding patterns or near final approach creates unworkable situation. Suspend tracks WITHOUT auto altitude readouts first. If still needed, suspend those WITH altitude readouts.

ARV = Approach Runway Verification. When alert received: evaluate reason, take appropriate action. Coordinate if another controller involved. Acknowledgment = action taken. Cannot suppress for another position without coordination or facility directive.
